title: Bridge Essay: Mind-wandering in Parkinson's disease hallucinations reflects primary visual and default network coupling.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Non-fabrication — spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, DMN, creative insight.
**Phase context:** Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, DMN, creative insight, involuntary cognition.

**Mind-wandering in Parkinson's disease hallucinations reflects primary visual and default network coupling.** — Cortex (2020). DOI: 10.1016/j.cortex.2019.12.023. PMID: 32058090. Visual hallucinations are an underappreciated symptom affecting the majority of patients during the natural history of Parkinson's disease. Little is known about other forms of abstract and internally generated cognition - such as mind-wandering - in this population, but emerging evidence suggests that an interplay between the brain's primary visual and default networks might play a crucial role in both internally generated imagery and hallucinations.
